title: [CI:DOCS] Update troubleshooting.md
body:
Add one more step `sudo systemctl daemon-reload` before re-login. Only with reloading systemd config fixing `--cpus` error on my situation.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Add one more step `sudo systemctl daemon-reload` before re-login.
```

Cockpit tests failed for commit 78012ed221e5285f621555c3c48825e68a26c07d. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Looks like the linter is complaining of trailing whitespace in your change

[rawhide failed](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/b1d38e91-e0e7-4a83-834e-7acf2400f093/) on a curious regression:
> error: Pod failed to be created: Internal Server Error: failed to make pod: building local pause image: building at STEP "COPY /usr/libexec/podman/catatonit /catatonit": storing The same happened in #22922 so it's not just an one-off -- and obviously not a direct cause from updating the docs here. podman devs, does that ring a bell? Could that be fallout from a non-podman package like containers-common, or some weird kernel/selinux thing? The [journal](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/b1d38e91-e0e7-4a83-834e-7acf2400f093/work-podman-system6j8qjnaz/plans/cockpit-podman/podman-system/execute/data/guest/default-0/test/browser/system-1/data/TestApplication-testCreatePodSystem-fedora-41-localhost-22-FAIL.log.gz) has a lot of SELinux things, but they are https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2282040 and I think unrelated. Otherwise it just has the error message from above, but no surrounding ones.

> The same happened in #22922 so it's not just an one-off -- and obviously not a direct cause from updating the docs here. podman devs, does that ring a bell? Could that be fallout from a non-podman package like containers-common, or some weird kernel/selinux thing? The [journal](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/b1d38e91-e0e7-4a83-834e-7acf2400f093/work-podman-system6j8qjnaz/plans/cockpit-podman/podman-system/execute/data/guest/default-0/test/browser/system-1/data/TestApplication-testCreatePodSystem-fedora-41-localhost-22-FAIL.log.gz) has a lot of SELinux things, but they are https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2282040 and I think unrelated. Otherwise it just has the error message from above, but no surrounding ones.

@martinpitt it is https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18543
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
